name: HRM-mlx
text:
Start by testing the stated equation for consistency.

For all real x and y,
f(x) + f(y) = f(x + y - xy).

Put x = 1. The argument on the right becomes
1 + y - 1*y = 1.

Thus
f(1) + f(y) = f(1).

Since the problem also states f(1) = 1, we get
1 + f(y) = 1,
hence f(y) = 0 for every real y.

This forces f(1) = 0, directly contradicting f(1) = 1. So the hypotheses cannot all be true; there is no valid function to evaluate at integers.

Final answer: no such function exists.
